commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: Add marketplace feed refresh command
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## SummaryAdds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh`, a small manual activation slice for the hosted marketplace feed stack. The command reads `config.marketplaces`, calls the configured hosted external plugin catalog loader, and reports whether the accepted catalog came from the hosted feed, the last-known-good snapshot, or bundled fallback.This gives Vince and maintainers a safe way to exercise the live `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` path and any locally configured `marketplaces.feeds` profile without changing startup, onboarding, doctor, install, or provider-repair behavior yet.## What changed- Adds `plugins marketplace refresh` with:  - `--feed-profile <name>` for a configured marketplace feed profile.  - `--feed-url <url>` for the existing guarded explicit hosted URL path.  - `--expected-sha256 <hash>` for checksum-pinned refresh checks.  - `--json` for automation-friendly source/feed/metadata output.- Keeps the command lazy-loaded with the existing plugin runtime boundary.- Reports hosted, hosted-snapshot, and bundled-fallback results.- Fails checksum-pinned refreshes when the accepted result is not a fresh hosted payload, so automation cannot silently accept bundled fallback after a checksum mismatch.- Adds focused tests for CLI routing, refresh output, fallback reporting, and pinned-refresh failure.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-config-profiles` initially found the pinned-refresh fallback exit issue; fixed in this PR and reran the same review clean: no actionable regressions found.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the first manual activation path for configured hosted marketplace feeds, including hosted/snapshot/bundled fallback reporting and redacted feed URL output.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-hosted-catalog-prep`, cumulative hosted marketplace feed stack through PR8 at `13cea8e00e7`, built source tree with `pnpm build`, isolated OpenClaw state via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX`, and the live ClawHub feed endpoint `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. `pnpm build`
3. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX node openclaw.mjs plugins marketplace refresh --feed-url https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins --json`
4. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX node openclaw.mjs plugins marketplace entries --feed-url https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`

Evidence after fix:
Focused tests passed across 3 Vitest shards: `1 passed / 8 tests`, `2 passed / 7 tests`, and `2 passed / 70 tests`. `pnpm build` completed successfully. Refresh exercised the bounded fallback path when the live endpoint exceeded the guarded timeout: `{ "source": "bundled-fallback", "entries": 74, "error": "request timed out" }` with `[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 5000ms ... url=https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`. A later built CLI entries call against the same live feed returned `Source: hosted`, `Entries: 59`, `Feed: clawhub-official sequence 9`.

Observed result after fix:
The refresh command reports fallback cleanly under a real timeout, and the same stack can consume live hosted ClawHub data when the guarded fetch completes.

What was not tested:
The live refresh command did not complete with `source: hosted` in this environment because repeated refresh attempts hit the 5000ms fetch guard. Hosted success is covered by focused refresh tests and by the built `entries` command against the same live feed.
